vocab.meaning: khiến ai đó phát điên/cực kỳ khó chịu
vocab.example: Sitting in stationary traffic for hours can drive me up the wall, so I usually try to distract myself with some upbeat music.

vocab.meaning: ngồi không, chẳng có việc gì để làm
vocab.example: Rather than just sitting there twiddling my thumbs while the cars crawl along, I prefer to listen to an educational audiobook.

vocab.meaning: quanh quẩn, tốn thời gian mà không tiến triển được gì
vocab.example: Sometimes I try to find a shortcut, but I often end up going around in circles and wasting more time than if I had stayed put.

vocab.meaning: gặm nhấm sự bực bội/tự chịu đựng hậu quả do mình chọn
vocab.example: Instead of just sitting in the car stewing in my own juice about being late, I call my colleagues to let them know the situation.

vocab.meaning: có một khoảng thời gian cực kỳ vui vẻ (thường dùng mỉa mai hoặc khi hát hò trong xe)
vocab.example: I actually have a whale of a time singing along to my favorite tracks at the top of my lungs when I'm stuck in a jam.

vocab.meaning: giữ tinh thần lạc quan (không nản lòng)
vocab.example: It's easy to get depressed in heavy congestion, but I try to keep my chin up by thinking about the delicious dinner waiting for me at home.
I’ve seen many drivers lose their cool and start honking incessantly, but I find that staying calm is much more productive.
Looking at the other frustrated drivers, I remind myself that we are all in the same boat, which somehow makes the wait feel less lonely.

vocab.word: on the edge of one's seat
vocab.meaning: hồi hộp, lo lắng (thường vì sợ trễ giờ)
vocab.example: I'm usually on the edge of my seat when there's a delay, constantly checking my watch to see if I’ll make it to my appointment.

vocab.meaning: chỉnh đốn lại, chuẩn bị sẵn sàng (tâm lý/công việc)
vocab.example: I use the slow movement of traffic to get my act together and mentally plan out the tasks I need to accomplish once I arrive.
